task_completion.description: GIMP is open with an image loaded
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible and an image is displayed in the main canvas area.

task_completion.description: The Offset dialog box is opened
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Offset' dialog window is visible on the screen (typically accessed via Layer → Transform → Offset).

task_completion.description: X and Y offset values are correctly entered
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Offset dialog, the X offset value is set to 100 and the Y offset value is set to 80.
Inside the Offset dialog, the 'Wrap around' option is selected under the Edge Behavior section (not 'Fill with background color').

task_completion.description: The offset transformation is applied to the image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dialog is closed and the image on the canvas shows the content shifted (100px horizontally, 80px vertically), with the overflowing parts wrapping around to the opposite edges without introducing solid colored borders.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with GIMP menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick) being executed.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripting to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the GIMP Python-Fu or Script-Fu console being used to execute the offset command programmatically.

integrity.description: The transformation was applied to the actual image
integrity.visual_evidence: The final result should be visible on the GIMP image canvas, not just as text in a document or a simulated mockup.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP. The correct menu path for the tool is Layer → Transform → Offset. The X offset must be exactly 100 and the Y offset must be exactly 80. The 'Wrap around' mode must be selected to ensure the image wraps seamlessly; if 'Fill with background color' is used, it will introduce solid borders (often black or white) which is incorrect. The final image should show the content shifted with the edges wrapping around to the opposite sides.
